title: Odd Sum Pair

content.problemStatement:
Chef has 333 numbers: AAA, BBB, and CCC.

Chef wonders if it is possible to choose **exactly** two numbers out of the three numbers such that their sum is **odd**. Your task is to help Chef determine if such a pair exists.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecheckOddPairscheckOddPairscheckOddPairs – This function checks if any two numbers out of the three can form an odd sum.

Parameters
AAA : an integer representing the first number.
BBB : an integer representing the second number.
CCC : an integer representing the third number.
Return ValueReturns a string: `"YES"` if it is possible to choose two numbers with an odd sum, otherwise returns `"NO"`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space-separated integers A, B and C.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if there exists a pair of numbers whose sum is odd, otherwise print "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 2 (even) + 3 (odd) = 5 odd → YES. Test case 2: all numbers are even → NO. Test case 3: all numbers are odd → NO. Test case 4: 7 (odd) + 8 (even) = 15 odd → YES.
All three numbers are even, so any pair sums to an even number.

hidden.label: stress case 4
hidden.expectedOutput:
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO
NO

solution.approach: A sum of two integers is odd iff one of them is even and the other is odd. Therefore we only need to know whether among the three numbers there is at least one even and at least one odd. Count the evens (or odds) and answer YES if both counts are non‑zero, otherwise NO. This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
